Heading: Census Survey Agent Guide
SubHeading: Quick Reference for Census Bureau Call Handlers

Summary.Heading: 🔐 Caller Verification
Summary.Description: Verify caller identity before providing information

Detail.Heading: Verification Procedures
Detail.Description: Steps to verify caller identity and Census record

Sections.TemplateString:
📋 Required Information

  • Census ID from mailing (12-digit code)
  • Full address on Census record
  • Head of household name
✓ Remember: Never ask for SSN, bank info, or payment

⚠️ Red Flags

  • Caller unable to verify any information
  • Asking about other households
  • Requesting information be sent to different address
  • Suspicious callback number requests

Summary.Heading: 🛡️ Privacy & Trust Concerns
Summary.Description: Address caller concerns about data protection

Detail.Heading: Privacy Protection Talking Points
Detail.Description: Key information to build caller trust

Sections.TemplateString:
📜 Legal Protections (Title 13)

  • 72-year rule: Individual responses sealed for 72 years
  • Cannot be shared: IRS, ICE, FBI, courts cannot access
  • Penalties: $250,000 fine + 5 years prison for violations
  • Statistical use only: Never used against individuals

🚫 What We NEVER Ask

  • Social Security numbers
  • Bank account or credit card info
  • Money or donations
  • Political party affiliation
  • Immigration status details

🔗 Verification Resources

Census Privacy Policy

About the Census

Verification line: 1-800-923-8282

Detail.Heading: Refusal Response Protocol
Detail.Description: Steps for when callers refuse to participate

Sections.TemplateString:
📋 Refusal Response Steps

  1. Acknowledge their concern respectfully
  2. Briefly explain Census importance (funding, representation)
  3. Offer alternative: paper form by mail
  4. Offer callback at more convenient time
  5. If still refusing, thank them and log reason

💡 Why Census Matters Script

"The Census determines congressional representation and over $1.5 trillion in federal funding for your community - including schools, roads, hospitals, and emergency services. Your response helps ensure your community gets its fair share."

📝 Common Refusal Reasons

ReasonResponse
Privacy concernsTitle 13 protections
Don't trust governmentStatistical use only
Too busyOffer callback/paper
Already completedVerify in system

  • Braille: Request form D-20BR

Summary.Heading: 🚨 Escalation Criteria
Summary.Description: When to involve specialists or supervisors

Sections.TemplateString:
🔴 Immediate Escalation

  • Caller reports Census impersonation/fraud
  • Threatening or abusive behavior
  • Media or press inquiries
  • Legal or Congressional inquiries
  • Data breach concerns

🟡 Specialist Transfer

  • Group quarters (dorms, nursing homes, prisons)
  • Complex household situations
  • Business/institutional surveys
  • Technical system issues
  • Formal complaints

📞 Transfer Queues

TypeQueue
General SpecialistCENSUS-SPECIALIST
Group QuartersCENSUS-GQ
Fraud/SecurityCENSUS-FRAUD
SupervisorCENSUS-SUP
